original_text:
### Typography Hierarchy (fonts, weights, cases)
| Level | Print | Digital / Email | Word / PPT / Excel fallback |
|---|---|---|---|
| Headlines & headers | **Agenda** (Adobe font) | **Nunito Sans** (Google font), bolder weights, Title Case; *Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only* | **Aptos** |
| Body copy | **Nunito Sans** | **Nunito Sans** | **Aptos** |
| Footers, fine print | Nunito Sans | Nunito Sans | Aptos |
 
- Available Agenda weights: Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold.
- Available Nunito Sans weights: Light, Regular, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold.
- Available Aptos weights: Regular, Bold.
- **[GENERAL] Email font stack (CSS):** `'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`. Nunito Sans must be loaded via `@font-face`/Google Fonts link where the client supports it; the stack guarantees graceful fallback in Outlook and other clients that strip webfonts.
- **[GENERAL] Email type scale (px / line-height):**
  - H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px (≈130%), Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold
  - H2 (section heading): 22px / 30px (≈136%), Nunito Sans Bold
  - H3 (subhead): 18px / 26px (≈144%), Nunito Sans Bold or SemiBold
  - Body: 16px / 24px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular — **16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better")**
  - Secondary/supporting body: 14px / 21px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular — use sparingly, never for primary content
  - Footnote/reference text within a section: 12px / 18px (150%) — must be "noticeably smaller" than primary data and clearly separated from it
  - Never set running text below 12px. (ISI sizing is owned by the locked component.)
- **Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy** (brand accessibility rule). **For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further** — [GENERAL] use 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).
- Use a clear **type scale / visual hierarchy** so the reader knows what to read first, next, last. Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.

relations.note: Type scale defines body font-size/line-height defaults (150%) that the line-height minimum rule further governs, including the dark-background exception.
The distinctness rule enforces the differentiation the type scale hierarchy is meant to create.
Both describe digital/email font handling for the same Nunito Sans family.

quotes: **Nunito Sans** (Google font), bolder weights, Title Case
Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only
Body copy | **Nunito Sans** | **Nunito Sans** | **Aptos**
Footers, fine print | Nunito Sans | Nunito Sans | Aptos
**Agenda** (Adobe font)
Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only
Body copy | **Nunito Sans** | **Nunito Sans** | **Aptos**

rules.intent: Keep font usage consistent per channel while reserving the Agenda display font for the special campaign headline treatment.
rules.rule_text: Headline/header font is Agenda in print and Aptos in Word/PPT/Excel, but in digital/email headlines use Nunito Sans (bolder weights, Title Case); Agenda may only be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline (campaign_headline.font = Agenda). Body copy and footers/fine print use Nunito Sans in print and digital, Aptos as the Word/PPT/Excel fallback.

quotes: 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
Nunito Sans must be loaded via `@font-face`/Google Fonts link where the client supports it; the stack guarantees graceful fallback in Outlook and other clients that strip webfonts.

rules.intent: Ensure consistent typography with reliable fallback across email clients.
rules.rule_text: Email CSS font stack is Nunito Sans, Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; Nunito Sans must be loaded via @font-face/Google Fonts link where supported, so the stack guarantees graceful fallback in Outlook and other clients that strip webfonts.

quotes: 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px (≈130%), Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold
H2 (section heading): 22px / 30px (≈136%), Nunito Sans Bold
H3 (subhead): 18px / 26px (≈144%), Nunito Sans Bold or SemiBold
Body: 16px / 24px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular
Secondary/supporting body: 14px / 21px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular
Footnote/reference text within a section: 12px / 18px (150%)
16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better")
use sparingly, never for primary content
must be "noticeably smaller" than primary data and clearly separated from it
Never set running text below 12px. (ISI sizing is owned by the locked component.)

rules.intent: Guarantee legible, clearly differentiated reading hierarchy across all email content.
rules.rule_text: The email type scale runs H1 28px/36px, H2 22px/30px, H3 18px/26px, Body 16px/24px, Secondary body 14px/21px, and Footnote 12px/18px, forming a strict visual hierarchy. Body copy's 16px is the brand-mandated minimum ('bigger is better'); secondary/supporting body (14px) must be used sparingly and never for primary content; footnote text must be noticeably smaller than primary data and clearly separated from it; running text must never drop below 12px (ISI sizing is owned by its locked component).

quotes: Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy
Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy
For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further

rules.intent: Maintain readability of body copy, with extra breathing room when contrast is reduced on dark backgrounds.
rules.rule_text: Body copy line height must be a minimum of 150% (brand accessibility rule); for light text set on dark backgrounds, increase further to 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).

quotes: Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.

rules.intent: Prevent ambiguous reading order by ensuring visually distinct hierarchy levels.
rules.rule_text: Maintain a clear type scale / visual hierarchy so readers know what to read first, next, last; never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size and weight to signal different hierarchy levels.
